query: Based on emissivity altitude behavior, what is the composition of the tallest Venus volcanoes?

candidates.title: Magellan SCVDR Emissivity Data File (EDF) Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Primary Magellan radiometry-derived surface emissivity measurements (SCVDR EDF) to examine how emissivity varies with elevation over Venus volcano summits/flanks.

candidates.title: Magellan Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Parent bundle for orbit-by-orbit surface characteristics (including emissivity estimates); useful for reproducing emissivity–altitude trends and linking to ancillary scattering parameters.

candidates.title: Magellan Global Vector Data Record (GVDR) Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Gridded summary products derived from Magellan SAR/altimetry/radiometry; useful for global mapping of emissivity-related parameters and comparison among volcanoes.

candidates.title: Magellan Venus Stereo-Derived Topography Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Elevation/topography needed to quantify the emissivity–altitude relationship over the tallest edifices (e.g., Maat Mons area) and to define summit elevations.

candidates.title: MGN-V-RDRS-5-GDR-EMISSIVITY-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Legacy PDS3 global emissivity grid/data record from Magellan radiometry; directly supports emissivity vs. elevation analyses.

candidates.title: MGN-V-RDRS-5-SCVDR-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Legacy PDS3 orbit-by-orbit Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record; includes derived emissivity and related scattering parameters to test altitude-dependent surface property changes.

candidates.title: MGN-V-RDRS-5-GDR-TOPOGRAPHIC-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Legacy PDS3 global topographic data record required to correlate Magellan emissivity with altitude over volcanoes.

candidates.title: MGN-V-RDRS-5-GVDR-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Legacy PDS3 Global Vector Data Record (gridded scattering/emission summaries); useful companion product for global context and cross-checking emissivity-related behavior.

tool_input.summary: To infer composition from Venus emissivity–altitude behavior, the key PDS holdings are Magellan radiometry-derived emissivity products (SCVDR/EDF and GDR-EMISSIVITY) combined with Magellan topography (GDR-TOPOGRAPHIC and/or stereo-derived topography). These let you reproduce the observed decrease in microwave emissivity at higher elevations and test whether volcano summits follow the same trend as other highlands.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
Based on emissivity altitude behavior, what is the composition of the tallest Venus volcanoes?

dataset.title: Magellan SCVDR Emissivity Data File (EDF) Data Collection
Magellan Venus Stereo-Derived Topography Data Collection
dataset.description: Stereo-derived topography produced from the Magellan stereo data.

dataset.title: Magellan Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) Bundle
dataset.description: The Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) is an orbit-by-orbit reduction of Magellan scattering and emission measurements carried out at Stanford University. The SCVDR includes near-nadir scattering functions obtained by numerical inversion from altimetry (ALT) echoes, results (e.g., rms surface slopes and Fresnel reflectivity) from fitting analytic functions to those inversions, scattering function segments at oblique incidence angles derived from synthetic aperture radar (SAR) echoes, and estimates of surface emissivity derived from thermal microwave radiometry (RAD) measurements. The SCVDR is one of several inputs to the Global Vector Data Record (GVDR), a gridded summary of scattering results, also produced at Stanford.

datasets.title: Magellan Global Vector Data Record (GVDR) Bundle
Magellan Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) Bundle

dataset.title: Magellan Global Vector Data Record (GVDR) Bundle
dataset.description: This bundle contains composite radar scattering functions derived from Magellan synthetic aperture radar, altimetry, and radiometry. Results are stored in ASCII and binary tables linked to pixels with 18.225 km resolution. Pixels are organized in sinusoidal equal area, Mercator, north polar stereographic, and south polar stereographic projections.
